models.name: Logistic Regression
models.label: Baseline
models.why_not: Linear decision boundary cannot capture non-linear interactions between PCA components. Limited by its inability to model the complex fraud signature patterns.
models.rationale: StandardScaler + LogisticRegression pipeline. Establishes the performance floor — interpretable and fast, but linear boundaries miss the complex fraud patterns in PCA-space.

models.name: XGBoost
models.label: Candidate 1
models.why_not: Default hyperparameters leave significant performance on the table. scale_pos_weight handles imbalance but learning rate and depth are not optimal for this feature space.
models.rationale: Gradient boosting with scale_pos_weight=599.5 for imbalance. Captures non-linear V-feature interactions. Industry standard for tabular fraud detection.

models.name: LightGBM
models.label: Candidate 2
models.why_not: Leaf-wise growth gives slightly lower precision than XGBoost on this dataset. Good recall but the AUPRC gap vs. XGBoost suggests this feature space favours level-wise splitting.
models.rationale: Fastest training (18.7s vs. 87s for RF). Native is_unbalance flag. Leaf-wise growth achieves better precision on rare classes — strong choice for latency-sensitive production.

models.name: Random Forest
models.label: Candidate 3
models.why_not: Bagging with independent trees misses sequential error correction that boosting provides. 5× slower to train with lower AUPRC — boosting methods clearly dominate on this dataset.
models.rationale: Bagging with class_weight='balanced'. Well-calibrated probabilities critical for AUPRC. Random feature subsets prevent V14 from dominating. Diversity comparison point.

models.name: XGBoost (Optuna-tuned)
models.label: Winner
models.why_won: 30-trial Optuna search on AUPRC found depth=10 + lr=0.12 + 287 estimators captures deep non-linear fraud patterns missed by default params. Lowest CV std (±0.016) confirms stability.
models.rationale: 30-trial Optuna optimization with AUPRC objective and 5-fold stratified CV. Best params: 287 estimators, depth=10, lr=0.12, subsample=0.80. Highest AUPRC with lowest variance — chosen for production.
